Prompt: Describe yourself today without using your job title or roles for others. Who are you at your core?
What is one negative thought you catch yourself saying often? Where did you learn it?
Take yesterday’s negative thought and rewrite it as a neutral or empowering statement.
List three things you did well today, no matter how small (e.g., 'I made a healthy lunch').
What is one piece of evidence from your life that contradicts your 'I'm not good enough' narrative?
List three things your body allows you to do that you are grateful for today.
Describe a time you felt good because you said 'no' to something that didn't serve you.
Who are you comparing yourself to? Identify one trait they have that you admire—how can you nurture that in yourself?
What is one hobby or activity you loved as a child? How can you incorporate a version of it into this week?
Look back at the last 10 days. What has surprised you most about your self-perception?
What are the three non-negotiable values you live by? Are your daily actions honoring them?
If your best friend came to you with your current struggle, what advice would you give them? Apply it to yourself.
Identify one small thing that scares you but would boost your confidence. Plan to do it this week.
Is there a past mistake you are still holding against yourself? Write a letter of forgiveness to your past self.
What activities or people drain your self-esteem? How can you distance yourself or limit that exposure?
Write about a time you stood up for your opinion. How did it feel?
What is a skill you possess that you often take for granted because it comes easily to you?
Describe what you look, act, and feel like when you are fully confident.
List a failure or hardship that helped you become more resilient.
If you had unlimited resources for your personal growth, what would you invest in first?
When you fail this week, what words will you use to soothe yourself instead of punishing yourself?
How does your physical space (home/desk) impact how you feel about yourself? What needs a refresh?
Identify one situation where you apologize when you don't need to. Practice owning your space instead.
Write a letter from your 'future self' (one year from now) telling you why they are proud of who you are today.
How do you want people to feel when they interact with you? How does that reflect your self-worth?
Create a 5-minute morning routine that makes you feel ready to take on the world.
What content (books, podcasts, social media) are you consuming? Does it build you up or tear you down?
Write your own definition of success that has nothing to do with productivity or external validation.
Look through your journal entries. What is the biggest shift you notice in your writing style?
Write a 'Self-Esteem Manifesto'—three promises you make to yourself to ensure you continue to treat yourself with respect.
